subject: A large serpentine Eldrazi creature with a segmented body, clawed limbs, and long trailing head-tendrils prowling through a submerged ruin environment
description: A large alien creature dominates the frame, rendered in deep blue-green tones. It has a long segmented body with ribbed or spined sections, multiple clawed limbs, and an elongated head from which long trailing tendril-like appendages extend. Its form is somewhere between a deep-sea crustacean and a serpentine predator. The environment is submerged or aquatic — ruined architectural fragments are visible in the background, suggesting it moves through flooded ruins. The creature's silhouette and anatomy are consistent with the Eldrazi aesthetic of BFZ: alien, unknowable, and utterly non-humanoid. Small fish-like forms appear in the lower portion of the frame for scale.
foreground: Large Eldrazi creature with segmented body and head tendrils
background: Submerged ruins in deep blue-green water
